demographics.living_situation: lives at home with primary caregiver

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (caregiver speaking) Their nose started pouring blood about an hour ago for no reason and I can't get it to stop, and they've got these dark purple bruises and little spots all over their legs even though they haven't bumped into anything.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The nosebleed won't stop and the purple spots and bruises are spreading — I'm scared about how much blood there is.

symptom_narrative.onset: the skin changes started about 24 hours ago; the nosebleed started suddenly about an hour ago
symptom_narrative.duration: bruising and spots over a day, nosebleed for about an hour and still going
symptom_narrative.character: big painless purple bruises on the shins, then lots of tiny pinpoint purple-red spots down both lower legs; now a steady ooze of blood from one nostril
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the nosebleed is heavy and won't stop even with pressure, and the spots keep appearing
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: bruises and spots mostly on the lower legs below the knees and some on the forearms; blood from the right nostril
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: coming on and spreading over the last day, nosebleed constant for the last hour
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: pressing on the nose slows it a little but it starts again
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: nothing they did — it all came on by itself
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no fever, no headache, not complaining of bone or joint pain, eating and drinking, still alert but resting quietly
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never had anything like this before; no easy bruising or bleeding in the past
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: sitting quietly holding a tissue to the nose, a bit frightened but able to talk

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a mild cold / runny nose about two weeks ago that got better on its own
normally a well, healthy child with no ongoing medical problems

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no ibuprofen, aspirin or other painkillers given recently

family_history_as_reported.narrative: no known family history of bleeding or clotting problems that the caregiver is aware of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: school-aged child at home with primary caregiver present; caregiver is very anxious about the amount of visible blood and the sudden rash

objective_data_offered.value: lots of tiny pinpoint purple-red spots over both lower legs below the knees, plus several large painless purple bruises on the shins and forearms; steady slow ooze of blood from the right nostril
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described/seen over video; the caregiver cannot measure how low the platelet count is or examine internally

objective_data_offered.value: child is alert and talking, sitting upright and leaning forward holding continuous pressure on the soft part of the nose; several blood-soaked tissues used already
temperature 36.9 degrees on our home thermometer
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: no fever, no drowsiness or confusion, no vomiting; the child says their head is fine and there is no tummy pain
